title: Sources of Domain Shift
concept: Domain shifts are caused by changes in camera sensors, resolution, lighting, geographic location, and styles.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is domain shift in computer vision?

options.text: When the statistical properties of the training data differ from the deployment data
When the model weights leak during code export
When the validation labels are saved in TXT format instead of JSON

exercises.explain: Domain shift represents the statistical gap between training (source) and real-world (target) data.

cards.title: Through different eyes
cards.body: To you, a car is a car, whether you look at it through a high-definition DSLR camera or a cheap security webcam. But to a convolutional network, these two cameras generate completely different pixel distributions. The source of the shift is in the lens.

cards.title: Sources of Shift
cards.body: Domain shifts stem from: Sensor Differences (lens distortion, resolution, color profiles), Environmental Changes (day vs. night, winter vs. summer), and Acquisition Shifts (simulated/synthetic data vs. real photos).

cards.title: Changing your glasses
cards.body: Imagine training to catch a ball while wearing normal glasses. Suddenly, someone replaces them with yellow-tinted sunglasses. The ball's shape is the same, but the colors, contrast, and glare have shifted. Your brain must adapt.

cards.title: Simulation-to-Real (Sim2Real)
cards.body: Robotic arms are often trained to grasp objects inside 3D virtual simulators (since simulated physics is fast and free). When the robot is placed in front of a real physical table, the shift in shadows and material textures (Sim2Real gap) often breaks the model.

cards.title: Pixels are not conceptual
cards.body: You might think a model understands 'edges' conceptually. In reality, edges are gradients of pixel values. If a new camera sensor softens those gradients slightly, the model's edge detectors may fail to trigger entirely.

cards.title: Visual Sources of Shift
cards.body: Comparing identical scenes under different sensor conditions:

left.text:
Clean, rendered pixels.
No sensor noise or motion blur.
Unlimited free data generation.

right.text:
Noisy pixels, grain, lens dust.
Natural shadows & reflections.
Hard to collect and annotate.

exercises.prompt: What is the 'Sim2Real' gap in robotics and vision?
exercises.explain: Sim2Real refers to the difficulty of getting models trained in simulation to work on real-world physical systems.

options.text: The domain shift between simulated training environments and the real physical world
The processing delay when sending images from GPU to CPU
The coordinate difference between bounding boxes and segmentation masks
The difference in file size between raw and compressed video streams

exercises.prompt: True or False: Upgrading your production camera to a higher resolution can sometimes trigger a domain shift that degrades a model trained on lower resolution.
exercises.explain: True. Changes in resolution, scaling, and sharpness change the texture features, which can confuse a model trained on low-res patterns.
Hardware sensor properties are a primary physical source of domain shift in deployed systems.
exercises.sentence: A camera sensor's color profile and lens distortion represent ___ sources of domain shift.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a geographic source of domain shift?
exercises.explain: Geographic shifts introduce new styles, markings, and cultural details that the model has never seen.

options.text: Deploying a traffic detector trained in America on streets in Tokyo (different road markings, car models, driving sides)
Training a model for double the number of epochs on the same American streets
Converting bounding boxes from COCO JSON to YOLO TXT format

exercises.prompt: You are building a forest fire detector. You train your model on high-resolution drone photos. In production, the model runs on cheap, grainy security cameras mounted on trees. What source of domain shift is most likely to cause failures?

options.text: Sensor shift (grainy, low-quality stationary camera pixels vs. clean, high-res drone photo pixels).
Data leakage from the test set.
A class mismatch between forest fires and drones.

exercises.explain: The difference in camera quality, noise, and angle represents a major hardware/sensor shift that changes the pixel representations.
